_comment: Taylor died in 1824 — all works public domain. Project Gutenberg has no Taylor entries. Use archive_downloader.py with the identifiers below, or the corpus/cleaned/ files already committed (downloaded March 2026 via Archive.org and Wikisource). Run: python execution/download_corpus.py --persona john-taylor-of-caroline
_digitization_note: Taylor is significantly less well-digitized than Hamilton or Jefferson. The correct Archive.org identifiers follow the Open Library naming convention (aratorbeingserie1813tayl), NOT the University of Toronto suffix pattern (arator00tayluoft). Tyranny Unmasked on Archive.org is a Liberty Fund reprint under controlled digital lending — retrieved from Wikisource instead. Definition of Parties (1794) not found in any accessible digitized form.

priority_1.title: An Inquiry into the Principles and Policy of the Government of the United States
priority_1.author: John Taylor of Caroline
priority_1.search_terms: Inquiry Principles Policy Government United States Taylor 1814
priority_1._note: Taylor's major philosophical work — 600+ pages of systematic argument against Hamilton's financial system and paper aristocracy. Direct reply to John Adams' Defence of the Constitutions.

priority_1.title: Arator: Being a Series of Agricultural Essays, Practical and Political
priority_1.author: John Taylor of Caroline
priority_1.search_terms: Arator Taylor Caroline agricultural essays political 1813
priority_1._note: Taylor's masterwork on agrarian political economy — interweaves practical farming advice with republican philosophy.

priority_1.title: Construction Construed and Constitutions Vindicated
priority_1.author: John Taylor of Caroline
priority_1._note: Taylor's constitutional argument against the Marshall Court's broad construction doctrine, particularly McCulloch v. Maryland.

priority_1.title: Tyranny Unmasked
priority_1.author: John Taylor of Caroline
priority_1._note: Retrieved from Wikisource (en.wikisource.org/wiki/Tyranny_Unmasked) — Preface, Section I, II, III. Archive.org copy (tyrannyunmasked0000tayl) is a Liberty Fund reprint under controlled digital lending; all file types return 403.

priority_1.title: New Views of the Constitution of the United States
priority_1.author: John Taylor of Caroline
priority_1.search_terms: New Views Constitution United States Taylor 1823
priority_1._note: Taylor's final major work — historical and textual argument that the Constitution was a compact of sovereign states.

priority_2.title: An Enquiry into the Principles and Tendency of Certain Public Measures
priority_2.author: John Taylor of Caroline
priority_2.search_terms: Enquiry Principles Tendency Certain Public Measures Taylor 1794
priority_2._note: Taylor's early extended attack on Hamilton's financial measures. Establishes the analytical framework he would develop at full length in the 1814 Inquiry.

priority_2.title: Taylor Correspondence — Tyler's Quarterly Historical and Genealogical Magazine
priority_2.author: John Taylor of Caroline et al.
priority_2.search_terms: Tyler's Quarterly Historical Genealogical Magazine Virginia letters
priority_2._note: Selected Taylor correspondence. Partial — full correspondence in manuscript collections at Library of Congress and Virginia Historical Society.

not_found.title: A Definition of Parties (1794)
not_found.author: John Taylor of Caroline
not_found._note: Not found in any accessible digitized form as of March 2026. May be available through Readex Early American Imprints (institutional subscription) or physical archives (AAS, Library of Congress).
